info.long_name: Total measles cases in the United States
info.short_description: Total measles cases reported in the United States for the given period.
info.long_description: Total count of confirmed measles cases in the United States. For rows where type='new_cases', this is the count of new cases for the reporting week. For rows where type='cumulative', this is the cumulative total from the start of the calendar year. Data is manually recorded from the CDC measles surveillance page.
info.statement: In the United States, {value} measles cases were reported as of {time}.

info.long_name: Measles cases in children under 5 years
info.short_description: Measles cases among children under 5 years of age.
info.long_description: Count of confirmed measles cases in the United States among children under 5 years of age. For type='new_cases', this is the weekly new case count; for type='cumulative', this is the year-to-date total. Negative values reflect CDC retroactive corrections.
info.statement: In the United States, {value} measles cases were reported in children under 5 as of {time}.

info.short_description: Measles cases among persons 5-19 years of age.
info.long_description: Count of confirmed measles cases in the United States among persons 5-19 years of age. For type='new_cases', this is the weekly new case count; for type='cumulative', this is the year-to-date total. Negative values reflect CDC retroactive corrections.
info.statement: In the United States, {value} measles cases were reported in persons 5-19 years as of {time}.

info.long_name: Measles cases in persons 20 years and older
info.short_description: Measles cases among persons 20 years and older.
info.long_description: Count of confirmed measles cases in the United States among persons 20 years and older. For type='new_cases', this is the weekly new case count; for type='cumulative', this is the year-to-date total. Negative values reflect CDC retroactive corrections.
info.statement: In the United States, {value} measles cases were reported in persons 20 and older as of {time}.

info.long_name: Measles cases in unvaccinated or unknown vaccination status persons
info.short_description: Measles cases among persons who were unvaccinated or had unknown vaccination status.
info.long_description: Count of confirmed measles cases in the United States among persons who were unvaccinated or whose vaccination status was unknown. This is a key indicator for tracking vaccine-preventable disease burden. Negative values reflect CDC retroactive corrections.
info.statement: In the United States, {value} measles cases were reported in unvaccinated or unknown vaccination status persons as of {time}.

info.long_name: Measles cases in persons who received 1 vaccine dose
info.short_description: Measles cases among persons who received exactly 1 dose of measles-containing vaccine.
info.long_description: Count of confirmed measles cases in the United States among persons who received exactly 1 dose of measles-containing vaccine. Negative values reflect CDC retroactive corrections.
info.statement: In the United States, {value} measles cases were reported in persons with 1 vaccine dose as of {time}.

info.long_name: Measles cases in persons who received 2 or more vaccine doses
info.short_description: Measles cases among persons who received 2 or more doses of measles-containing vaccine.
info.long_description: Count of confirmed measles cases in the United States among persons who received 2 or more doses of measles-containing vaccine. Negative values reflect CDC retroactive corrections.
info.statement: In the United States, {value} measles cases were reported in persons with 2 or more vaccine doses as of {time}.

info.long_name: Total measles-associated hospitalizations in the United States
info.short_description: Total measles-associated hospitalizations reported in the United States.
info.long_description: Total count of measles-associated hospitalizations in the United States. For type='new_cases', this is the count of new hospitalizations for the reporting week. For type='cumulative', this is the year-to-date total.
info.statement: In the United States, {value} measles-associated hospitalizations were reported as of {time}.

info.long_name: Measles hospitalizations count in children under 5 (new cases only)
info.short_description: Count of measles hospitalizations in children under 5 (available for new weekly cases only).
info.long_description: Count of measles-associated hospitalizations in children under 5 years of age. This column is only populated for type='new_cases' rows; it is NA for type='cumulative' rows (which instead report hosp_pct_under_5). Negative values reflect CDC retroactive corrections.

info.long_name: Measles hospitalization percentage in children under 5 (cumulative only)
info.short_description: Cumulative percentage of measles hospitalizations in children under 5 (available for cumulative rows only).
info.long_description: Percentage of all measles-associated hospitalizations occurring in children under 5 years of age. This column is only populated for type='cumulative' rows; it is NA for type='new_cases' rows (which instead report hosp_count_under_5).

This is a key indicator for tracking vaccine-preventable disease burden. Negative values reflect CDC retroactive corrections.

cases_one_dose.long_name: Measles cases in persons who received 1 vaccine dose
cases_one_dose.short_description: Measles cases among persons who received exactly 1 dose of measles-containing vaccine.
cases_one_dose.long_description: Count of confirmed measles cases in the United States among persons who received exactly 1 dose of measles-containing vaccine. Negative values reflect CDC retroactive corrections.
cases_one_dose.statement: In the United States, {value} measles cases were reported in persons with 1 vaccine dose as of {time}.

hosp_total.long_name: Total measles-associated hospitalizations in the United States
hosp_total.short_description: Total measles-associated hospitalizations reported in the United States.
hosp_total.long_description: Total count of measles-associated hospitalizations in the United States. For type='new_cases', this is the count of new hospitalizations for the reporting week. For type='cumulative', this is the year-to-date total.

hosp_count_under_5.long_name: Measles hospitalizations count in children under 5 (new cases only)

hosp_count_under_5.long_description: Count of measles-associated hospitalizations in children under 5 years of age. This column is only populated for type='new_cases' rows; it is NA for type='cumulative' rows (which instead report hosp_pct_under_5). Negative values reflect CDC retroactive corrections.

hosp_pct_under_5.long_name: Measles hospitalization percentage in children under 5 (cumulative only)
hosp_pct_under_5.short_description: Cumulative percentage of measles hospitalizations in children under 5 (available for cumulative rows only).
hosp_pct_under_5.long_description: Percentage of all measles-associated hospitalizations occurring in children under 5 years of age. This column is only populated for type='cumulative' rows; it is NA for type='new_cases' rows (which instead report hosp_count_under_5).

cdc_measles_age2.name: CDC Measles Cases and Outbreaks - Age and Vaccination Status
cdc_measles_age2.organization: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
cdc_measles_age2.description: Weekly new cases and cumulative case counts for measles in the United States, stratified by age group (under 5, 5-19, 20+, unknown) and vaccination status (unvaccinated/unknown, 1 dose, 2+ doses), along with hospitalization data. The new cases file provides weekly counts with hospitalization counts per age group. The cumulative file provides year-to-date totals with hospitalization percentages per age group. Data is updated weekly on the CDC Measles Cases and Outbreaks surveillance page. Negative values in case counts reflect retroactive corrections by the CDC. Cases are classified as confirmed or probable following CSTE case definitions.
cdc_measles_age2.restrictions: Public domain. CDC data is generally not subject to copyright restrictions.
